Ratified by the National Consultative Assembly (Majlis) 28 May 1933, Royal assent following day -- replaced the original 1901 D'Arcy Concession after the Anglo-Persian Oil Company's 1901 concession was formally cancelled by Reza Shah in Nov 1932 amid the royalty dispute
Duration extended from the original 1961 expiry (1901+60yr) to 1993
Fixed royalty replacing the vague 16%-of-net-profits formula, protecting Persia against oil-price fluctuations
Persia also entitled to 20% of the company's worldwide distributed profits above a specified minimum sum
Guaranteed minimum annual payment irrespective of production/profit levels
